text.title: Prompt Versioning and A/B Framework Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns from real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Eval results don't pin to prompt version. Regressions impossible to attribute. Postmortem stalls.
antipattern.root-cause: Code references prompts by name without version pin.
antipattern.fix: Mandate `prompt_id@version` in every reference; lockfile holds the active version per environment.

antipattern.symptom: Same user sees variant A and variant B alternately; user-level metrics dominated by within-user noise.
antipattern.root-cause: Traffic split implemented as `random() < 0.5` per request.
antipattern.fix: Switch to hash(user_id) % 2 == 0 → variant_a; deterministic per user.

antipattern.symptom: Test launched with n=200. Effect size needed n=5000. Result inconclusive after weeks of traffic.
antipattern.root-cause: Power analysis skipped at design time.
antipattern.fix: Pre-launch checklist: power analysis + MDE + target n; tests without it blocked.

antipattern.symptom: Variant promoted because eyeball suggests it 'reads better'. No metric supports. A regresses on production.
antipattern.fix: Significance gate at p<0.05 OR Bayesian posterior >0.95; promotion script rejects below threshold.
